verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But perhaps mankind seems to them to be of less value than these senseless objects.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: They bring down the substance of God into stones and into dogs; but many heretics into fouler things than these.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But we could never endure even to hear of these things.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But what we say is that of a virgin’s womb the Christ took pure flesh, holy and without spot, and made impervious to all sin, and restored the body that was His own.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: A little further on: “And we assert that when the divine Word had fashioned for Himself a holy temple by its means he brought the heavenly state into our life.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Of the same from the oration: That the lowly words and deeds of Christ were not spoken and done through lack of power, but through distinctions of dispensation.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: “What then are the causes of many humble things having been said about Him both by Himself and by His apostles?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: The first and greatest cause is the fact of His having clothed Himself with flesh, and wishing all his contemporaries and all who have lived since, to believe that He was not a shadow, nor what was seen merely a form, but reality of nature.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For if when He Himself and His apostles had spoken about Him so often in humble and in human sense, the devil yet had power to persuade some wretched and miserable men to deny the reason of the incarnation, and dare to say that He did not take flesh and so to destroy all the ground of His love for man, how many would not have fallen into this abyss if He had never said anything of the kind?”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: I have now produced for you a few out of many authorities of the heralds of the truth, not to stun you with too many.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: They are quite enough to show the bent of the mind of the excellent writers.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: It is now for you to say what force their writings seem to have.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—They have all spoken in harmony with one another, and the workers in the vineyard of the West agree with them whose husbandry is done in the region of the rising sun.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Yet I perceived a considerable difference in their sayings.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—They are successors of the divine apostles; some even of those apostles were privileged to hear the holy voice and see the goodly sight.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: The majority of them too were adorned with the crown of martyrdom.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Does it seem right for you to wag the tongue of blasphemy against them?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I shrink from doing this; at the same time I do not approve of their great divergence.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—But now I will bring you an unexpected remedy.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: I will adduce one of your own beautiful heresy—your teacher Apollinarius, and I will shew you that he understood the text “The Word was made flesh” just as the holy Fathers did.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Hear now what he wrote about it in his “Summary.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: The testimony of Apollinarius from his “Summary”:—

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: “If no one is turned into that which he assumes, and Christ assumed flesh, then He was not turned into flesh.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And immediately afterward he continues:—

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: “For also He gave himself to us in relationship by means of the body to save us.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Now that which saves is far more excellent than that which is being saved.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Far more excellent then than we are, is He in the assumption of a body!

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But He would not have been more excellent had He been turned into flesh.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: A little further on he says:—

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: “The simple is one, but the complex cannot be one; he then that alleges that He was made flesh affirms the mutation of the one Word.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: But if the complex is also one, as man, then he who on account of the union with the flesh says the Word was made flesh means the one in complexity.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And again a little further on he says—“To be made flesh is to be made empty, but the being made empty declares not man, but the Son of man, who ‘emptied Himself’ not by undergoing change, but by investiture.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: There; you see the teacher of your own doctrines has introduced the word ‘investiture’ and indeed in his little work upon the faith he says—“We then believe that he was made flesh, while His Godhead remained unchanged for the renewal of the manhood.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For in the holy power of God there has been neither alteration nor change of place, nor inclusion”—and then shortly again—“We worship God who took flesh of the blessed virgin, and on this account in the flesh is man, but in the spirit God.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: And in another exposition he says—“We confess the Son of God to have been made the Son of man, not nominally but verily, on taking flesh of the Virgin Mary.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—I did not suppose that Apollinarius held these sentiments.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: I had other ideas about him.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Well; now you have learnt that not only the prophets and apostles, and they who after them were ordained teachers of the world, but even Apollinarius, the writer of heretical babbling, confesses the divine Word to be immutable, states that He was not turned into flesh but assumed flesh, and this over and over again, as you have heard.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Do not then struggle to throw your master’s blasphemy into the shade by your own.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: For, says the Lord “the disciple is not above his master.”

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Yes, I confess that the divine Word of God is immutable and took flesh.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: It were the uttermost foolishness to withstand authorities so many and so great.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Do you wish to have a solution of the rest of the difficulties?

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Eran.—Let us put off their investigation until to-morrow.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Orth.—Very well; our synod is dismissed.

verses.book: Dialogues. The “Eranistes” or “Polymorphus” of the Blessed Theodoretus, Bishop of Cyrus.
verses.text: Let us depart, and bear in mind what we have agreed upon.
